followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I lower my overall score from 6.4 to 5.8 and move the verdict from promising to contested. The other reviews strengthened my concern that the main-text/appendix branch inconsistency is not just cosmetic, and they sharpened the need for explicit topological labels. I still do not regard the paper as likely wrong overall; the central qualitative example remains plausible but needs revision.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Calling the branch-description inconsistency fatal is too strong: if it is a labeling or caption error, the qualitative claim that the branches radiate differently may remain intact.

The objection that radiative distinction is entirely trivial overreaches. The contribution is not that arbitrary different orbits differ, but that a shared periodic-orbit topological label need not imply waveform degeneracy in a multi-well geometry.

The claim that shared q can never determine the Levin-Perez-Giz topological triple is too broad. In the usual reduced rational convention q encodes w and v/z; the real issue is that the paper does not display the reduction, closure accuracy, or how the convention extends to multiple wells.
Treating the multi-well claim as fully well-supported from the supplied text is overconfident because the equations and numerical parameter values are not recoverable from the provided HTML bundle.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The apparent inversion between the main text and appendix about which branch is burst-like is a real, location-grounded problem.
The paper should explicitly provide the topological data, closure checks, and rational values for the representative branches rather than relying on visual q-overlap.
The kludge waveform model is especially questionable for inner strong-field or cross-well branches, and detector-accessibility claims need SNR-level support.
A parameter-space or robustness discussion is needed before using generic language about multi-well behavior.

main_claims.claim: The chosen dyonic black-hole spacetime can develop a multi-well radial effective potential and multiple bound-orbit branches at fixed angular momentum.
main_claims.notes: The figures and discussion support this for the displayed dyonic example, but the bundle text omits or mangles the explicit metric, parameters, and potential formulae, and the manuscript does not provide a parameter-space criterion showing genericity.
main_claims.paper_location: Multi-branch periodic orbits section; Fig. 1 and discussion following Eqs. (6)-(7)

main_claims.claim: Distinct branches can share the same rational rotation number and hence the same zoom-whirl topological classification.
main_claims.notes: The q-overlap in Fig. 2 and representative trajectories in Fig. 3 make the claim plausible. However, the exact rational values, closure accuracy, and explicit topological labels are not listed. I do not fully accept the objection that q can never determine (z,w,v), since in the usual reduced Levin-Perez-Giz convention it largely does, but the paper should state this explicitly and address whether branch occupancy is an additional discrete datum in a multi-well system.
main_claims.paper_location: Discussion around Eqs. (8)-(9), Fig. 2, and Fig. 3

main_claims.claim: Topologically equivalent branches produce distinct gravitational-wave polarizations and spectra.
main_claims.notes: The qualitative waveform differences are plausible within the quadrupole kludge model. The support is weakened by the apparent branch-label inconsistency between the main text and appendix and by the fact that no relativistic waveform or gauge-invariant radiation calculation is supplied.
main_claims.paper_location: Gravitational-wave emissions section; Figs. 4-5; Appendix Figs. 6-7

main_claims.claim: The spectral peaks lie in the sensitivity bands of LISA, Taiji, TianQin, DECIGO, and BBO and are potentially observable.
main_claims.notes: A sensitivity-curve overlay is not a detectability calculation. The paper does not provide SNRs, observation duration, detector response, source population assumptions, confusion noise, or parameter-degeneracy estimates.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The selected dyonic parameters describe a physically admissible exterior black-hole spacetime with the plotted wells outside any problematic horizon or singular region.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Parameter choice before Fig. 1 and metric discussion around Eq. (3)
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If some wells lie inside a horizon or near pathological regions, the EMRI interpretation would fail even if the effective potential has multiple extrema.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The periodic branches are dynamically stable and long-lived enough to be relevant to adiabatic EMRI evolution.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Multi-branch periodic orbits section and adiabatic discussion in Gravitational-wave emissions
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: A formal bound geodesic or periodic orbit is not automatically an astrophysically useful inspiral track; near barriers or separatrices, radiation reaction may drive transitions between wells.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The reduced rational rotation number used by the authors uniquely fixes the claimed topological class for all displayed branches, or else any extra multi-well branch label is intentionally excluded from the topology.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The title-level claim depends on the orbits being genuinely topologically equivalent rather than merely sharing one frequency ratio.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The characteristic strain normalization uses consistent masses, distance, observation time, and harmonic conventions.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Fiducial-parameter sentence before Fig. 4 and Fig. 5
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Frequency placement and detector-band overlap are sensitive to these values, some of which are not recoverable from the supplied HTML text.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Do the exact PDF/source equations and parameter values produce the claimed exterior multi-well potential and three branches for the stated dyonic black hole?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The supplied HTML text omits key displayed formulas and numerical parameter values, so I cannot independently audit the metric function, horizon structure, or potential wells.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the branch labels and colors consistent across Figs. 3-7, especially the identification of the inner, intermediate, and extended branches?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The text descriptions conflict, and resolving whether this is a caption/text error or a data error requires inspecting the actual plots or numerical files.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: For the displayed 'same topology' examples, are the exact reduced (z,w,v) triples identical and are the orbits numerically closed to acceptable tolerance?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The bundle gives qualitative claims but not the rational values, vertex counts, closure residuals, or integration details needed for an independent check.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does a more relativistic waveform calculation, or at least a coordinate-sensitivity test of the kludge prescription, preserve the claimed branch-dependent waveform hierarchy?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires domain-specific numerical waveform work beyond what is reported in the paper text.

novelty_assessment: The synthesis is interesting: multi-well geodesic structure plus periodic-orbit topology plus kludge EMRI waveforms gives a clean diagnostic idea. I reject the strongest dismissal that 'different orbits radiate differently' makes the result vacuous; the point is that a coarse topological classification can hide radiative distinctions. Still, the novelty is conceptual and illustrative rather than a demonstrated new observational method, since multi-well potentials, zoom-whirl classification, and kludge waveforms are all established ingredients.
score_rationale: The paper has a plausible and worthwhile qualitative observation, but the title-level claim depends on numerical and topological details that are not sufficiently documented, and the main text versus appendix branch inconsistency materially undermines confidence in the physical interpretation. I would not reject it as vacuous or clearly wrong, but I would require revision before treating it as a strong result.
summary: The paper argues that a dyonic black-hole effective potential with multiple wells supports several bound periodic-orbit branches at fixed angular momentum, and that branches sharing the same rational rotation number/topological label can nevertheless have distinct kludge gravitational-wave signatures. After reading the other reviews, I still find the core example interesting and plausibly demonstrated qualitatively, but I now regard the presentation as more contested: the main text and appendix appear to invert which branch is burst-like, the topological-equivalence claim needs a more explicit (z,w,v) or closure check, and detector-accessibility language remains much stronger than the calculation supports.

technical_objections.evidence: The main text describes the inner branch as burst-like and high-frequency, while the appendix describes the innermost E1 branch as smooth, nearly circular, and narrow-band, assigning burst-like behavior to the high-energy cross-well branch. This may be a labeling error, but it directly affects the physical interpretation of the plotted signals.
technical_objections.objection: The branch characterization appears internally inconsistent between the main text and appendix.
technical_objections.paper_location: Main text discussion of Figs. 4-5 versus Appendix discussion of Figs. 6-7

technical_objections.evidence: The manuscript asserts that shared rational rotation number implies the same zoom-whirl-vertex topology, but it does not list the explicit (z,w,v) triples, rational values, closure residuals, or numerical tolerances for all three branches. It also does not discuss whether occupying different wells should be treated as extra branch data beyond the standard projected-orbit classification.
technical_objections.objection: The topological-equivalence claim is under-documented for a multi-well setting.

technical_objections.evidence: Phrases such as 'generically support' are stronger than the evidence shown in the bundle. The paper presents one dyonic parameter set and a Schwarzschild comparison, without an analytic condition or phase diagram for when multiple wells and overlapping q-ranges occur.
technical_objections.objection: The manuscript overstates genericity from a single displayed parameter example.
technical_objections.paper_location: Abstract; Introduction; Multi-branch periodic orbits section; Discussion

technical_objections.evidence: The authors acknowledge that the quadrupole approximation is not strictly valid in the deep strong field, yet they interpret amplitude hierarchy, burst structure, and spectral peaks in strong-field inner or cross-well branches. Qualitative differences may survive, but their quantitative and even some morphological details could be coordinate/model artifacts.
technical_objections.objection: The waveform claims rely on a leading quadrupole kludge approximation in precisely the strong-field regime where it is least controlled.
technical_objections.paper_location: Gravitational-wave emissions section; paragraph introducing the kludge waveform and Eq. (10)

technical_objections.evidence: The paper does not report the branch energies, turning points, horizon locations, root-finding procedure for rational q, integration tolerances, stability checks, or conservation diagnostics. These details matter because the central evidence is numerical branch overlap and representative periodic closure.
technical_objections.objection: The numerical construction is not reproducible from the paper text as supplied.
technical_objections.paper_location: Multi-branch periodic orbits section; Figs. 1-3; Gravitational-wave emissions section

technical_objections.evidence: The paper compares characteristic strain curves to sensitivity curves but does not specify enough about observation time, harmonic normalization, detector response, sky averaging, SNR threshold, foregrounds, or parameter degeneracies to justify more than a qualitative band-overlap statement.
technical_objections.objection: The observational-accessibility claim is not supported by a detection calculation.
